tldr: Lea, 6 of your 7 live ads are running the exact same 'All U.S. Orders Ship and Return from Texas' + '15% off your first purchase 🤍' combo with the headline and body literally swapped between variants. Expect frequency to spike and CPMs to climb 25-40% within 10-14 days as Meta caps delivery on near-duplicates.

benchmark.context: Luxury outerwear competitors typically rotate 4-6 distinct creative angles (product hero, founder story, fabric close-up, styling). Your pool is essentially one shipping/discount message duplicated 6 ways, which is why fatigue is hitting roughly 2x faster than category median.

ads.headline: All U.S. Orders Ship and Return from Texas
drivers: Identical headline + body copy as ads at INDEX 1, 2, and 5 — four-way duplicate competing with itself in auction
Logistics line ('Ship and Return from Texas') is doing the hook work where a product or fabric story should be
All traffic dumped to /collections/leather-jackets — no landing page rotation across the entire pool

ads.headline: All U.S. Orders Ship and Return from Texas
drivers: Carbon copy of INDEX 0, 2, 5 — Meta will throttle one or more in the same ad set within days
Discount-led body cheapens a luxury cashmere/fur positioning Lea built the brand on
Zero hook variance to test what actually drives clicks — shipping vs. discount vs. product

ads.headline: All U.S. Orders Ship and Return from Texas
drivers: Third instance of the identical creative — frequency on warm audiences is almost certainly above 4.0
No founder voice or 'L.CUPPINI established London 2019' story anywhere in the pool
CTA 'Shop now' used in 5 of 7 ads — no urgency or specificity tested

ads.headline: Take 15% off your first purchase 🤍
ads.body: All U.S. Orders Ship and Return from Texas
drivers: Same two lines as INDEX 0/1/2/5 just flipped — Meta's creative similarity detector treats this as a near-duplicate
Discount-first headline trains the audience to wait for promos instead of buying at full price
Landing page identical to all 6 other ads — leather jackets only, hiding the cashmere and outerwear catalog

ads.headline: Nationwide Shipping & Returns
ads.body: All U.S. Orders Ship and Return from Texas
drivers: Only ad in the pool with a different headline, but the body still repeats the Texas shipping line for the 7th time
Pure logistics message with no product, no price, no aspiration — won't carry cold prospecting
Same /leather-jackets landing as every other ad — wasted opportunity to send to Linda Tux Blazer or cashmere

hero_concept.hook: The blazer Lea designed to outlast every trend.
hero_concept.headline: Linda Tux Blazer, Beige
hero_concept.primary_text: When Lea Lindaas started L.CUPPINI in London in 2019, the goal was simple: outerwear that doesn't expire. The Linda Tux Blazer in beige is cut for the woman who's done chasing seasons — tailored shoulder, soft hand, weight that holds its shape after the tenth wear. Free express shipping worldwide on orders over £600.
hero_concept.visual_direction: The Linda Tux Blazer Beige photographed on a minimalist wooden hanger against a warm off-white plaster wall, soft morning light, editorial luxury feel.
hero_concept.fills_gap: All 7 live ads lead with shipping logistics or a 15% discount — none feature a specific product, fabric story, or the founder's London origin. This concept introduces product-led, full-price luxury positioning that the entire pool is missing.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a minimalist Parisian atelier setting on a slim wooden hanger against a warm off-white plaster wall with a single sculptural ceramic vase nearby, with soft directional morning light from a tall window casting gentle shadows across the fabric weave. Muted neutral palette of cream, beige, and warm taupe with a quiet, timeless luxury aesthetic that feels closer to a Céline lookbook than a discount ecommerce ad. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. Maintain the exact appearance, color, and details of the product from the reference image. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no text, no logos, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no buttons, no faces, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products.

next_step.urgency: 4 of your 7 ads hit fatigue between May 7-9 — under a week out.
next_step.headline: Get the May refresh plan on a 30-min call
next_step.body: Lea, I'll walk you through a 5-creative replacement slate built around Linda Tux, the cashmere line, and your London founder story — full-price positioning, no shipping-line crutch. We'll map launch order against the ads dying first so spend doesn't dip.

method_note: Scored from public Meta Ad Library creative signals; does not include account-level frequency, CTR, or ROAS data.
